case.caption: Northstar Construction, Inc. v. Pelham Properties, LLC
case.summary: Northstar, the general contractor on a $4.25M renovation/seismic retrofit at 1488 Folsom St., sues Pelham for unpaid Draws #15 and #16 ($954,800), foreclosure of mechanic's lien, and account stated. Pelham answers and counterclaims for breach of contract, negligent construction, and breach of warranty, alleging deficient seismic anchoring and rooftop-deck waterproofing defects with $1.42M in remediation costs. Parties dispute the date of substantial completion (Oct 22, 2023 vs. Jan 30, 2024) and whether the work conformed to spec.

parties.name: Superior Court of California, County of San Francisco

events.label: Construction Agreement signed
events.detail: Northstar and Pelham executed a written Construction Agreement for the renovation and seismic retrofit of 1488 Folsom Street; total price $4,250,000 payable through monthly progress draws. Section 3.1 required workmanlike performance per approved plans; § 12.3 governed notices of defective work; § 14.2 provides for attorneys' fees.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Establishes contractual obligations including § 12.3 30-day cure mechanism and § 14.2 fee-shifting

events.label: Northstar mobilizes / demolition begins
events.detail: Northstar mobilized on site and commenced demolition work.

events.label: Change Order No. 1 issued
events.detail: Pelham issued Change Order No. 1 expanding the scope of work to include relocation of mechanical systems; agreed cost impact $312,400.

events.label: Change Order No. 2 issued
events.detail: Pelham issued Change Order No. 2 adding a rooftop deck and related structural reinforcement; agreed cost impact $487,800.

events.label: Pelham raises seismic-anchoring concerns
events.detail: Beginning in March 2023 Pelham raised concerns about deviations from the approved seismic anchoring specifications via correspondence with Northstar. Pelham asserts the concerns were not adequately addressed.

events.label: Pelham retains Wexford
events.detail: Pelham retained Wexford Structural Engineering to perform an independent inspection of the seismic anchoring work.

events.label: Wexford report identifies 12 deviations
events.detail: Wexford issued a written report identifying twelve specific deviations from the seismic anchoring spec.

events.label: Wexford report forwarded to Northstar
events.detail: Pelham forwarded the Wexford report to Northstar.

events.label: Northstar disputes Wexford findings
events.detail: Northstar issued a written response disputing the Wexford report's findings.

events.label: Resolution meeting (no agreement)
events.detail: The parties met to attempt resolution of the Wexford findings; no agreement was reached.

events.label: Draw #14 paid
events.detail: Pelham timely paid progress Draw #14 — the last draw paid under the Contract.

events.label: Substantial completion (Northstar version)
events.detail: Northstar asserts it substantially completed the work on October 22, 2023, on which date a Notice of Substantial Completion was issued.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Triggers final-payment and warranty clocks; affects timing of mechanic's lien recording and accrual of breach claim

events.label: Draw #15 submitted ($342,500)
events.detail: Northstar submitted Draw #15 in the amount of $342,500 with documentation; Pelham refused to pay.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Alleged unpaid amount underlying breach claim

events.label: Owner walkthrough finds defects
events.detail: After Northstar's purported completion, Pelham conducted an owner walkthrough and identified continuing seismic anchoring deficiencies and new defects in the rooftop deck waterproofing. Additional owner walkthroughs occurred through December 2023.

events.label: Draw #16 (Final) submitted ($612,300)
events.detail: Northstar submitted Draw #16 (Final) in the amount of $612,300 following completion of punch list items; Pelham refused to pay.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Final invoice; combined with Draw #15 totals $954,800 sued for

events.label: Notice of Defective Work issued (§ 12.3)
events.detail: Pelham issued a formal Notice of Defective Work to Northstar pursuant to Section 12.3 of the Contract; Northstar did not cure within the contractual 30-day period.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Triggers 30-day contractual cure period under § 12.3; nonperformance underlies counterclaim

events.label: Mechanic's Lien recorded ($954,800)
events.detail: Northstar recorded a Mechanic's Lien against the Property in the amount of $954,800.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Pelham challenges validity for noncompliance with Cal. Civ. Code § 8416

events.label: 14-day notice of default & demand
events.detail: Northstar sent Pelham a 14-day notice of default and demand for payment. Pelham did not respond.
events.jurisdictional_significance: Pre-litigation demand; supports breach and account-stated claims

events.label: Substantial completion (Pelham version)
events.detail: Pelham contends substantial completion was not achieved until at least January 30, 2024, due to deficient seismic anchoring discovered during owner walkthroughs in November and December 2023.
events.jurisdictional_significance: If Jan 30 controls, Northstar's Oct 30 Draw #15 and Dec 1 Draw #16 were premature, and the Dec 18 mechanic's lien recording may be defective for timing

events.label: Complaint filed
events.detail: Northstar filed Complaint for (1) breach of contract, (2) foreclosure of mechanic's lien, and (3) account stated, seeking not less than $954,800 plus fees and interest. Jury trial demanded.

events.label: Answer filed
events.detail: Pelham filed Answer admitting the contract, mobilization, change orders, and submission of Draws #15-16, but denying substantial completion on Oct 22, 2023 and asserting affirmative defenses of failure of consideration / material breach, offset, and lien invalidity under Cal. Civ. Code § 8416.

events.label: Counterclaim filed
events.detail: Pelham filed Counterclaim for (1) breach of contract, (2) negligent construction, and (3) breach of warranty, seeking $1,420,000 in compensatory damages, disgorgement of amounts paid for non-conforming work, and discharge of the mechanic's lien.

relationships.detail: Change Order No. 1 modifies the underlying Contract
Change Order No. 2 modifies the underlying Contract
Pelham retained Wexford in response to unaddressed seismic anchoring concerns
Northstar's Aug 25 letter responds to the forwarded Wexford report
Northstar submitted Draw #15 eight days after its asserted substantial completion
Pelham's walkthrough findings are offered to contradict Northstar's claim that the work was substantially complete on Oct 22
Notice of Defective Work triggered § 12.3 30-day cure period; Pelham alleges Northstar did not cure
Northstar recorded lien after Pelham refused to pay Draws #15 and #16
Complaint filed after Pelham did not respond to 14-day demand
Pelham's Answer responds to Northstar's Complaint
Counterclaim filed with Answer in the same pleading

disputes.subject: Date of substantial completion of the construction work

versions.basis: Notice of Substantial Completion dated Oct 22, 2023 (Compl. ¶ 9, Ex. D)
Answer ¶ 4 — owner walkthroughs in Nov-Dec 2023 revealed deficient seismic anchoring; completion not achieved until at least Jan 30, 2024

disputes.materiality: Controls timing of final payment obligation, validity of mechanic's lien (Cal. Civ. Code § 8416 timing), accrual of warranty period, and whether Pelham's refusal to pay Draws #15 and #16 was wrongful
